tldr: Lob is a direct (physical) mail API, not an email/SMS provider, so this is a channel switch rather than a like-for-like migration — only do it if you're replacing digital messages with printed mail.
why_switch: Reach recipients via physical mail when email deliverability or engagement is poor
Programmatic postcards, letters, and checks via API
Useful for compliance documents, KYC, or high-value retention pieces
Adds an offline channel; does not replace transactional email at parity
what_to_export: Audit of which Brevo flows you actually want delivered as physical mail
Recipient name + postal address fields (these may not exist in your current Brevo data)
Templates that need to be redesigned for print (PDF/HTML print layouts)
Brand assets (logo, fonts) at print resolution
Existing Brevo templates and contact attributes for reference
Compliance and consent records for postal contact
export_method: From Brevo, export contacts and templates via the official API or dashboard — consult Brevo's current documentation. Most Brevo contact records will not include verified postal addresses, so plan an address-collection or address-verification step before any Lob send.
import_method: Create a Lob account, set up an API key, and rebuild templates as print-ready HTML/PDF per Lob's documentation. Verify and standardize addresses through Lob's address verification flow before sending. Hook delivery and tracking webhooks into your existing event pipeline. Start with a small test batch before scaling up.

concept_mapping.note: Different channel, different latency and cost profile
Page size, bleed, and resolution requirements differ
Run through address verification before sending
Maintain a separate suppression list for physical mail
Tracking semantics differ; no opens or clicks
Lob is a sending API, not an automation platform

gotchas: Physical mail has per-piece printing and postage cost — far higher than email
You almost certainly need to collect and verify postal addresses you don't have today
Delivery latency is days, not seconds — flows assuming instant delivery must be redesigned
Print artwork has strict size, bleed, and resolution requirements
Do not retire Brevo until you confirm Lob covers the underlying use case (most likely it doesn't fully)
Regional availability and postage rates vary; confirm in the official Lob documentation

email.subject: Reframing the Brevo → Lob 'migration'
email.body:
Hi team,

A note before we start: Lob is a physical mail API (postcards, letters, checks), so it isn't a drop-in replacement for Brevo's email/SMS sending. We should treat this as adding or swapping a channel, not as a 1:1 migration.

If the goal is genuinely to move some flows to direct mail, we need to: collect/verify postal addresses, redesign templates for print, set up Lob webhooks, and run a small pilot. For the email and SMS work currently in Brevo, we'd still need a separate plan if we're moving off Brevo at all.

Can we align on which specific flows are in scope before I write the runbook?

Thanks
